title: Ocugen Q2 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Ocugen Q2 2021 Earnings Call Summary. COVAXIN Progress: Ocugen expanded its Canadian rights through an amendment with Bharat Biotech and submitted a regulatory filing to Health Canada. The company is in active discussions with the FDA regarding a Biologics License Application (BLA), with management indicating confidence in their ability to navigate and address the FDA's process and requirements. Clinical Data: Bharat Biotech released Phase 3 data showing COVAXIN achieved 77.8% overall efficacy and 93.4% efficacy against severe COVID-19. The trial demonstrated 65.2% effectiveness against the Delta variant. Manufacturing: Ocugen selected Jubilant HollisterStier as the contract manufacturer for North American production, with technology transfer from Bharat Biotech underway. Financial Highlights: Cash position: $115.8 million (vs. $24.2 million at year-end 2020). Q2 R&D expenses: $18.9 million (included $15 million upfront payment for Canadian rights). Net loss: $26 million ($0.13 per share). Gene Therapy Pipeline: The company remains on track to initiate its first Phase 1-2 gene therapy trial for OCU400 later in 2021, targeting rhodopsin and NR2E3 mutations, with IND filing expected by Q4.
